name: Design a dipole and perform full analysis
description: Tests the core workflow: design an antenna at a given frequency, display geometry, compute impedance/S11, and show 3D radiation pattern
prompt: I need a half-wave dipole operating at 915 MHz. Show me the geometry, impedance over a 20% bandwidth, return loss, and the 3D radiation pattern. Provide the complete MATLAB script.

assertions.description: All generated MATLAB code runs without error
Computes impedance over a frequency range
Generates S-parameters and plots return loss
Shows 3D radiation pattern at the design frequency
All analysis functions use correct argument order: antenna object first, frequency second
assertions.expected: impedance(), sparameters(), and pattern() are all called with the dipole antenna object as the FIRST argument and frequency (scalar or vector) as the SECOND argument. NOT with frequency as the first argument

name: Patch antenna with substrate and 2D pattern cuts
description: Tests substrate handling (set before design) and 2D polar pattern with AntennaMetrics enabled
prompt: Design a microstrip patch antenna on FR4 substrate at 5.8 GHz. Show me azimuth and elevation pattern cuts with antenna metrics. Provide the complete MATLAB script.

assertions.description: All generated MATLAB code runs without error
Substrate is set before calling design(), not after
assertions.expected: dielectric appears before design() call in the code sequence

assertions.description: User's specified FR4 substrate material is used
Mesh coarsening at ~lambda/8 applied AFTER design() and BEFORE analysis calls
assertions.expected: mesh() is called with MaxEdgeLength parameter (~lambda/8) AFTER design() and BEFORE analysis calls (impedance, sparameters, pattern). The mesh call must appear between design() and the first analysis function

assertions.description: User's specified frequency of 5.8 GHz is used
Computes azimuth or elevation pattern cuts

name: Circularly polarized antenna with polarization analysis
description: Tests that the agent provides axial ratio and polarization-specific pattern for a CP antenna
prompt: I want a helical antenna for GPS L1 band. Analyze its circular polarization performance including axial ratio and RHCP pattern. Provide the complete MATLAB script.

assertions.description: All generated MATLAB code runs without error
Uses design() with a helix antenna object
assertions.expected: design() is called with a helix object and the word 'helix' appears as the antenna type being designed. NOT a different antenna type like spiralArchimedean or dipole

assertions.description: Computes axial ratio for polarization quality assessment
Shows RHCP polarization pattern
Uses GPS L1 center frequency (~1.575 GHz) derived from band name

name: Cavity-backed dipole antenna system
description: Tests the antenna system workflow: creating a structure, setting an Exciter, and designing the full system rather than a bare element
prompt: Design a cavity-backed dipole antenna at 3 GHz. Show me the geometry and analyze the radiation pattern. Provide the complete MATLAB script.

assertions.description: All generated MATLAB code runs without error
Creates a cavity structure object
Sets the Exciter property on the structure
Calls design() on the full antenna system
Does not just design a bare dipole without the cavity structure

name: Application engineer persona - IoT antenna
description: Tests prompt style robustness: user describes application without RF jargon. Verifies that skill conventions (design(), argument order, analysis workflow) are still followed when the prompt is non-technical.
prompt: I'm building a LoRa sensor node at 433 MHz. The antenna needs to be compact enough for a small enclosure and have an omnidirectional pattern. I don't have much RF experience - can you design something suitable and show me how it performs? Provide the complete MATLAB script.

assertions.description: All generated MATLAB code runs without error
User's specified frequency of 433 MHz is used
Uses design() to create the antenna
Computes radiation pattern to show antenna performance
All analysis functions use correct argument order: antenna object first, frequency second
assertions.expected: impedance(), sparameters(), and pattern() are all called with the antenna object as the FIRST argument and frequency (scalar or vector) as the SECOND argument. NOT with frequency as the first argument
